children.text: the term “appropriate Federal banking agency” has the meaning given the term in section 2 of the Economic Growth, Regulatory Relief, and Consumer Protection Act ( note); and12 U.S.C. 5365

the terms “Community Bank Leverage Ratio” and “qualifying community bank” have the meanings given the terms in section 201(a) of the Economic Growth, Regulatory Relief, and Consumer Protection Act ( note).12 U.S.C. 5371

children.label: A
children.text: the Community Bank Leverage Ratio shall be 8 percent; and

children.label: B
children.text: a qualifying community bank that falls below the Community Bank Leverage Ratio established under subparagraph (A) shall have a reasonable grace period to satisfy the Community Bank Leverage Ratio.

children.label: A
children.text: the termination date of the national emergency concerning the novel coronavirus disease (COVID–19) outbreak declared by the President on under the National Emergencies Act ( et seq.); orMarch 13, 202050 U.S.C. 1601

children.heading: Grace period
children.label: c Grace period
children.text: During a grace period described in subsection (b)(1)(B), a qualifying community bank to which the grace period applies may continue to be treated as a qualifying community bank and shall be presumed to satisfy the capital and leverage requirements described in section 201(c) of the Economic Growth, Regulatory Relief, and Consumer Protection Act ( note).12 U.S.C. 5371
